scenario: J: BREACH ALERT - Found privileged container 'cryptominer' running in prod-payments namespace. Security team is rotating credentials. Generate the Gatekeeper/Conftest Rego policy to prevent privileged containers from ever deploying again.
J: Incident detected - attacker used hostNetwork:true to sniff traffic between pods. While IR team investigates, write the OPA policy to block any pod that requests host network access.
J: Supply chain attack confirmed - nginx:latest was replaced with backdoored image. Create Rego policy that denies any container using :latest tag or no tag at all. This goes into Conftest for CI.
J: Post-mortem finding - attacker pushed malicious tag to same name. We need immutable image references. Generate policy requiring @sha256: digest for all container images.
J: Container escape detected via /proc access. Attacker used hostPID:true. Generate the deny policy to block hostPID namespace sharing.
J: IPC-based data exfiltration found. Pod was using hostIPC:true to access shared memory. Write Rego to deny hostIPC access.
J: Root escape exploit used in breach. Container was running as UID 0. Create policy denying runAsUser: 0 or missing runAsNonRoot: true.
J: Cryptojacking detected - pods consuming 100% CPU, no limits set. Resource exhaustion DoS. Generate policy requiring CPU limits on all containers.
J: OOMKill cascade caused outage. No memory limits allowed runaway process. Write policy requiring memory limits.
J: Data breach via hostPath mount to /etc/shadow. Attacker exfil'd credentials. Generate policy denying hostPath volume mounts.
J: Lateral movement in default namespace. No isolation. Create policy blocking deployments to 'default' namespace.
J: Container used allowPrivilegeEscalation to gain root. Write policy denying allowPrivilegeEscalation: true.
J: CRITICAL - Found docker.sock mount giving container access to Docker daemon. Full cluster compromise possible. Generate policy denying /var/run/docker.sock mounts.
J: Malware persisted by writing to container filesystem. Immutable infrastructure needed. Write policy requiring readOnlyRootFilesystem: true.
J: Container escape via CAP_SYS_ADMIN. Full node access achieved. Generate policy denying SYS_ADMIN capability.
J: Zombie containers ran for weeks undetected. No health checks. Create policy requiring livenessProbe on all containers.
J: Traffic routed to unready containers causing errors. Write policy requiring readinessProbe.
J: Incident response failed - couldn't identify pod owners. No labels. Generate policy requiring 'app' and 'owner' labels.
J: SSRF attack used auto-mounted SA token to access K8s API. Write policy denying automountServiceAccountToken: true.
J: Syscall-based exploit. No seccomp profile. Generate policy requiring seccomp profile (RuntimeDefault or Localhost).

J: This policy isn't working - pods still deploying without error messages. Fix this Rego:
```rego
package kubernetes.admission
deny[msg] {
    input.request.kind.kind == "Pod"
    container := input.request.object.spec.containers[_]
    container.securityContext.privileged == true
}
```

J: This policy is backwards - it's denying pods WITH CPU limits instead of without. Fix the logic:
```rego
package kubernetes.admission
deny[msg] {
    input.request.kind.kind == "Pod"
    container := input.request.object.spec.containers[_]
    container.resources.limits.cpu
    msg := sprintf("Container '%s' must define CPU limits", [container.name])
}
```

J: Policy won't load - OPA says 'rego_parse_error'. What's wrong with this?
```rego
deny[msg] {
    input.request.kind.kind == "Pod"
    not input.request.object.metadata.labels["app"]
    msg := "Pods must have 'app' label"
}
```

J: This crashes on pods without securityContext. Fix the nil pointer issue:
```rego
package kubernetes.admission
deny[msg] {
    input.request.kind.kind == "Pod"
    container := input.request.object.spec.containers[_]
    container.securityContext.runAsUser == 0
    msg := "Container runs as root"
}
```

J: Rego syntax error - can't concatenate strings. Fix this:
```rego
package kubernetes.admission
deny[msg] {
    input.request.kind.kind == "Pod"
    container := input.request.object.spec.containers[_]
    not container.resources.limits.cpu
    msg := "Container " + container.name + " missing CPU limit"
}
```
J: Scheduler placing pods poorly - no resource requests defined. Cluster overcommitted. Write policy requiring cpu and memory requests.
J: Stale cached images used after compromise. Need Always pull. Generate policy requiring imagePullPolicy: Always.
J: Lateral movement possible - no network segmentation. Create policy requiring NetworkPolicy exists before pod deployment.
J: Service exposed with externalIPs bypass - traffic hijacking. Write policy denying externalIPs on Services.
J: AppArmor could have prevented this exploit. Generate policy requiring AppArmor profile annotation.
